content:
# Session 24 — Iter-014 Prover Round (Path-2 Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite)

## Metadata

- **Archon iteration**: 014 (canonical) — Path-2 Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite scaffold sub-step. The blueprint and informal-prose surfaces still carry the legacy `iter-013` label — that is the plan-agent's editorial domain and not the review agent's surface to rewrite. Per the prompt, this session is iteration **014**.
- **Session number**: 24 (prover-round counter — sessions 22 / 23 / 24 cover iter-012 / iter-013 / iter-014 respectively; the legacy iter-013 label inside the blueprint chapter conflicts with the canonical iter-014 number Archon uses for *this* session, but I keep both straight per the prompt).
- **Stage**: prover (single-lane, on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`)
- **Sorry count before this session**: 10 (9 baseline + 1 scaffold sorry inserted by the iter-014 plan/refactor pass at L232–L238).
- **Sorry count after this session**: 9 (back to baseline: 5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`; the file `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` has dropped off the sorry list entirely again).
- **Targets attempted**: 1 (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'`).
- **Targets solved**: 1.
- **First-edit closure rate**: 100% (extends the streak to **eight** consecutive prover rounds — sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24).
- **New `axiom` declarations**: 0.
- **Files edited**: 1 (`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`).
- **Pre-processed events** (`attempts_raw.jsonl`): 19 events — 1 edit (the body closure), 3 diagnostic checks (all clean — `success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []`), 1 `lean_verify` axiom check (kernel-only), 2 `lean_run_code` smoke tests (both `success: true`), 1 `Genus.lean` regression compile (clean), 1 `archon-protected.yaml` git-diff check (empty), 1 task-result write, 4 file reads, 5 incidental tool / search calls. No goal-state queries (body was probe-confirmed by the iter-014 plan agent and dropped in verbatim).

## Targets

### Target 1 — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'`

**File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, lines 232 (signature start) – 238 (body).
**Status**: SOLVED (first-edit, verbatim probe-confirmed body).
**Significance**: Iter-014 lands the `ModuleCat k`-flavored *cohomology of an open*, parallel to Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X` (file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean`, L105) for `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves but with `ModuleCat.free k` in place of `AddCommGrp.free`. This is the **prerequisite** carrier for the iter-015+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`) and feeds the Čech-vs-derived-functor comparison theorem queued for iter-016+. Functorially / mathematically it is `Ext^n((free-k-module-on-yoneda(X))^♯, F)`.

#### Goal at the sorry site (post-iter-014-refactor)

```lean
noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u := sorry
```

The signature was set up by the iter-014 plan agent + refactor agent with `[HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)]` added (in addition to the `[HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]` already required by `HModule`) so that `presheafToSheaf` can sheafify the free-module-on-yoneda construction. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) follows the iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`: instance synthesis of `Module k (HModule' k F n X)` and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` requires the wrapper to be reducible, otherwise `Module.finrank` would fail to typecheck.

#### Attempt 1 (succeeded)

- **Strategy**: Adopt the probe-confirmed one-line term-mode body verbatim from the iter-014 plan-agent directive. The directive explicitly allowed either `Ext` or the qualified `Abelian.Ext` spelling; the prover chose the qualified form for stylistic consistency with the iter-009 `HModule` body at L185–L190 and the iter-010 `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` body at L203–L210.
- **Code tried** (`Edit` event, `attempts_raw.jsonl` log line 8, file `StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, body block at L236–L238):

  ```lean
  -- old_text
      (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u := sorry

  -- new_text
      (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
    Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
      ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
  ```

- **Lean error**: none. `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the post-edit file (log line 10) returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — zero errors, zero warnings. The `declaration uses 'sorry'` warning cleared.
- **Goal before**: term-position `⊢ Type u` under binders `{k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))] (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C)`.
- **Goal after**: closed.
- **Result**: success.
- **Insight**: The expression elaborates as follows. (i) `yoneda : C ⥤ (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u)` is the Yoneda embedding into the presheaf-of-types category. (ii) `(Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k)` post-composes a presheaf with the free-`k`-module functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k`. (iii) Composing (i) and (ii) yields a functor `C ⥤ (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k)`; applying it to `X : C` gives a presheaf of `k`-modules. (iv) `presheafToSheaf _ _` sheafifies it (uses `[HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)]` plus `[HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]`). (v) `Abelian.Ext _ F n` then gives the `n`-th `Ext` group. The `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility surfaces `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances on the result through the `Linear k` enrichment chain — verified by the smoke test `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1 X) : ℕ` at log line 15.

#### Verification

- **Compilation** (`lean_diagnostic_messages` on `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, log line 10): `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — clean.
- **Axioms** (`lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'`, log line 11): `{axioms: ["propext", "Classical.choice", "Quot.sound"], warnings: []}` — only standard kernel axioms. **No new `axiom` declarations**, no `sorryAx`.
- **Sorry count** (sorry-analyzer, log line 12): `9 total across 3 file(s)` — 5 in `Jacobian.lean`, 3 in `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 in `Picard/Functor.lean`. **`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` no longer appears.** Matches the iter-014 directive's `10 → 9` expectation exactly.
- **Smoke test** (`lean_run_code`, log line 15): `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1 X) : ℕ` typechecks against the closed file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`. The `noncomputable abbrev` reducibility surfaces the `Module k` instance correctly.
- **`Genus.lean`** (`lean_diagnostic_messages` log line 16, 18; `lean_run_code` log line 19): clean — zero errors, zero warnings. The iter-011 honest closure of `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` remains intact.
- **`archon-protected.yaml`** (git-diff log line 17): empty — untouched.
- **Forbidden shortcuts**: none used. The body is the genuine `Abelian.Ext` of the genuine `presheafToSheaf` of the genuine `yoneda ⋙ whiskeringRight ModuleCat.free k` construction, against the actual sheaf `F`. No vacuous types (`PUnit`, `ULift PUnit`, `ModuleCat.of k PUnit`), no constants, no `Module.finrank` placeholder, no new file scaffolded, no new `axiom` declarations, no rebuild of upstream iter-006/009/010 declarations, no signature changes.

## File-level verification (post-prover-round)

- **Sorry count**: `9 total across 3 file(s)` — 5 in `Jacobian.lean`, 3 in `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 in `Picard/Functor.lean`. Matches expectation.
- **`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`**: zero sorries, zero errors, zero warnings. The iter-006 toModuleKSheaf chain, iter-009 HModule, iter-010 HModule_zero_linearEquiv, iter-012 cechCochain_OC / cechCohomology_OC, and iter-014 HModule' all coexist cleanly.
- **`Genus.lean`**: clean (iter-011 honest closure intact).
- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: untouched.
- **Forbidden shortcuts**: none used.

## Key findings / proof patterns (this session)

- **Eight-consecutive-round 100% first-edit closure streak** (sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24). Probe-confirmed bodies for definitional-equality / single-Mathlib-application closures continue to land verbatim. Iter-014 was a single scaffold-target round, with the body again a one-liner — pattern matches iter-009/010/011 closures (single Mathlib-call wrapper around `Abelian.Ext`-and-friends).
- **Mathlib's `presheafToSheaf` accepts `ModuleCat.{u} k` as a value category** (this session). The `presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` sheafification needs the typeclass `[HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]` and additionally `[HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)]` to elaborate the underlying-type sheafification step. Both were added to the iter-014 signature.
- **`yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)`** is the standard Mathlib spelling for "the free-`k`-module presheaf on the Yoneda-representable presheaf at `X`" (this session). Elaborates cleanly when `yoneda : C ⥤ (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u)` and `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` are in scope. Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X` pattern for `AddCommGrp.free`.
- **`Abelian.Ext` (qualified) vs `Ext` (unqualified) is style-only** when `open CategoryTheory` is active (re-confirmed iter-014). The directive explicitly allowed either; the prover chose the qualified form for stylistic consistency with iter-009 / iter-010.
- **`noncomputable abbrev` continues to be the right shape for instance-transparent wrappers around `Ext`** (iter-009, iter-014 both use this; iter-010, iter-011, iter-012 use `noncomputable def` because they are value-style consumed declarations, not instance-transparent wrappers — pattern documented since session 15).
- **Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe correctness rate is now 100% on eight consecutive rounds** (sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24). For narrow-scope rounds with single-Mathlib-call closures, "adopt and verify" with no mid-edit budget is correct.
- **The `iter-013` label inside the blueprint chapter is the plan-agent's editorial choice and predates the canonical Archon iter-014 numbering for *this* prover round** (this session). The prompt explicitly declares iter-014 the canonical number for this session; the chapter's `(iter-013)` titling on the section header and definition block is the plan agent's surface and not the review agent's to rewrite. Markers (the review agent's surface) are kept correct independent of the section title.

## Recommendations for next session

See `recommendations.md` for the actionable briefing for the iter-015 plan agent. Headline: with the `ModuleCat k`-flavored cohomology-of-an-open carrier (`HModule'`) now defined (this session), the leading priority is **the `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES** on a `MayerVietorisSquare` — a direct mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` for `AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, substituting `HModule'` for `Sheaf.H'`. Iter-016+ then follows with the Čech-vs-derived-functor comparison on an acyclic cover and the affine-finiteness step. The 8 remaining protected sorries plus the deferred `representable` are all gated behind multi-iteration upstream work; do not assign as direct prover objectives.

## Blueprint markers updated

- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, `def:Scheme_HModule_prime` (definition block, ≈L343–L352): added `\leanok` to the statement block — the Lean declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'` exists with the directive's signature and the file compiles cleanly (verified `lean_diagnostic_messages` `success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []`).
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, proof block of `def:Scheme_HModule_prime` (≈L354–L362): proof-block `\leanok` was already in place at L355 from the iter-014 plan/refactor scaffold pass; **re-verified that the marker is now correctly attached to an honestly-closed body** (no `sorry`, kernel-only axioms `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`, no warnings, no errors). Marker remains in place, now valid.
- No `\lean{...}` macro renames required this session — the prover round did not rename or move any declaration; the name `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'` matches the directive verbatim.
- No stale `\notready` markers anywhere in the blueprint chapters (verified via grep).
- The `(iter-013)` section title and definition title inside `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` are the plan-agent's editorial surface — not rewritten by the review agent. The canonical iter-014 number is recorded only in this journal and in `recommendations.md` / `PROJECT_STATUS.md`.
